mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a linear regression against FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.5%, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or Rank IC is negative over a 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with expanding industrial production signals synchronized demand and supply recovery, which forces equity valuations higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and INDPRO move in opposite directions, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and real GDP indicates a broad demand contraction that suppresses corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns during late-cycle expansions, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a walk-forward test across expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate capacity utilization gains that boost short-term corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero correlation with INDPRO changes, or the signal's Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 over a 36-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL exceeding the 0.4% threshold, or the signal's hit rate falls below 40% during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside a stable or declining federal funds rate confirms a soft landing trajectory that supports equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when payrolls rise but rates remain elevated, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial output and rising housing inventory signals supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict short-term drawdowns, or forward returns remain positive in >60% of observed divergence cases during recovery phases.
